summary: HIP stream concurrency is correctly implemented for Q+Qgate and K+V GEMV projections. Two streams are created at engine init, used for concurrent projection launches, and synchronized before QK-norm. Launch count reaches 11 per full-attention layer (well within the <=12 target).

notes: _init_streams() creates two streams (_stream_q, _stream_kv) at engine init; falls back gracefully (streams=0) if creation fails but prints warning.
Q+Qgate GEMV launched on _stream_q, K+V GEMV on _stream_kv.
Both streams synchronized with hipStreamSynchronize before _launch_qknorm_rope calls that depend on both.
d_normed is read-only during both GEMVs — race-free design is sound.
Launch counting infrastructure: _record_launch() tracks per-layer counts when _count_launches=True.
Cleanup: _stream_q and _stream_kv are properly destroyed in engine.cleanup().
VAL-DLR-008: 11 launches per full-attention layer verified (vs baseline 16-19).
VAL-DLR-009: stream synchronization pattern confirmed via test.
Tests verify stream creation, decode correctness with streams, launch count (11 <= 12), no race conditions (5 steps all finite), and proper cleanup.
Decode output with streams matches without streams (max_err=0) — deterministic.
